commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=344f80b3ee0c9b287953e8a66d211147844769d6)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `RUSTUP_HOME` and `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN` to the shared host-env blocked override policy, reported baseline, generated macOS mirror, and sanitizer tests.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +14, Generated +4. Total +22 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible, not live-reproduced: current main lacks `RUSTUP_*` in the blocked policy, while the PR adds them to a policy bucket that also drives inherited stripping. I did not run a live rustup command in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer decision on inherited rustup compatibility plus contributor real behavior proof; this is not a safe autonomous repair while the desired policy is unresolved.

target.title: fix: block rustup toolchain env overrides [AI]
target.body:
## Summary
- Block rustup toolchain relocation variables in host exec environment overrides.
- Intentionally strip inherited rustup relocation variables through the shared host-env policy derivation.
- Extend reported host-env baseline coverage for the new blocked keys and regenerate the macOS policy mirror.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: agent/request supplied and inherited `RUSTUP_HOME` / `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN` no longer survive host exec environment sanitization.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node/Vitest through the repo test wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:host-env-policy:swift`

Evidence after fix:
- `src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts` asserts both keys are dangerous override names, appear in `rejectedOverrideBlockedKeys`, and are absent from sanitized override output.
- `src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts` also asserts inherited `RUSTUP_HOME` and `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN` are stripped from sanitized host env.
- `src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts` covers the updated reported baseline through inherited and override sanitizer paths.

Observed result after fix: 3 focused Vitest files passed, 34 tests passed; generated Swift policy check reported `OK apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/HostEnvSecurityPolicy.generated.swift`.

What was not tested: no live rustup toolchain execution was run; the sanitizer boundary is covered directly at the policy and host-env merge point.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:host-env-policy:swift`

AI-assisted: yes
target.author: pgondhi987
